name: Argentinian Asado
tagline: Grilled ribeye, salt only — Argentina's reverence for beef

hero.alt: Charred grilled ribeye with grill marks on a wooden board
hero.caption: asado — beef and salt

steps.title: Temper the beef
steps.detail: Pull steaks from fridge 45 min before cooking. Cold steak in a hot grill ends in raw center.
steps.tip: Tempering is mandatory for thick steaks — otherwise the outside burns before the center warms.

steps.title: Build the fire
steps.detail: Light a charcoal or wood fire and let it burn down to glowing embers (no flames). You want radiant heat, not jet flame.
steps.tip: Argentine parrillas are wood-fired — gas works but won't smell the same.

steps.title: Salt and grill
steps.detail: Generously coat both sides with coarse salt. Lay steaks on the grate over medium-hot embers. Cook 6 min per side for medium-rare.
steps.tip: Don't move the steak — the only flip is the one to the other side.

steps.title: Rest and slice
steps.detail: Rest steaks 10 min on a board. Slice across the grain into thick pieces. Eat with chimichurri or just bread and red wine.
steps.tip: Resting redistributes juices — slicing too soon loses them all.

healthFacts: Reverse-sear keeps thick steaks pink edge to edge.
Coarse salt draws moisture, then dries — perfect crust.
